mechanism_note: Ethylene induces SlbHLH25 and SlbHLH25-like and sustains PR gene expression in resistant cultivars
notes: High-priority bacterial-resistance signature because the axis is specific and mechanistically explicit.

mechanism_note: Free SA content rises alongside disease resistance and PR1-related signaling
notes: Useful anchor marker because SA is both observed and mechanistically grounded.

mechanism_note: Loss of callose synthase abolishes the observed resistance gain, marking callose as a determinant signature
notes: Core barrier marker for any compact plant-defense panel.

mechanism_note: Resistance and susceptibility are shaped through differential control of SA, JA, and ethylene pathways
notes: Important fungal-context marker because it prevents the panel from becoming bacteria-biased.

signature_name: PR proteins and receptor kinase activation
mechanism_note: Resistant genotype shows stronger enrichment of PR proteins, receptor kinases, MAPK, and hormone signaling pathways
notes: High-value comparative record because it links resistance phenotype to a coordinated defense program.

mechanism_note: Resistant material shows defensive enrichment of flavonoids and phenolic acids tied to phenylpropanoid metabolism
notes: Good bridge between transcript signatures and metabolite outputs.

mechanism_note: WRKY15 suppresses PAD3 and GSTU4, reducing camalexin synthesis and antifungal resistance
notes: Key metabolite-side signature that keeps the panel from being only hormone-centered.
